minimum_distinct_people.reason: CP3 requires independent facilitation or jurisdiction/professional review outside the minimum three-person operating core.

internal_role_clusters.title: Program and recruitment coordination
conditions: Consent approval must have an independent reviewer
No recruitment authority over a dependent or coerced participant

internal_role_clusters.title: Safeguarding and facilitation
conditions: Another reviewer handles complaints concerning that person's action
Not the participant's coercive supervisor, evaluator, or disputed representative

internal_role_clusters.title: Evidence, privacy, and access
conditions: High-impact privacy, deletion, incident, or export decisions require a second approver
Evidence custodian cannot close an incident concerning their own access action

internal_role_clusters.title: Jurisdiction and professional qualification
conditions: May be external to the standing team
Must document jurisdiction, scope, competence, limits, and expiry

absolute_separation_rules.rule: The subject of a complaint or concern may not be the sole investigator, approver, or closer.
A disputed representative or possible source of harm may not act as the sole facilitator or safeguarding reviewer.
The recruiter or compensation owner may not be the sole consent approver for a dependent participant.
The evidence custodian may not be the sole reviewer of a privacy, access, deletion, or export incident caused by their own action.
The program owner may not be the sole independent facilitator for an affected-person pilot.
Jurisdiction and professional approval may not be issued outside documented competence.
